title_en: Star Wars Zero Company Is Now Available to Preorder Ahead of Its Release in August – Here’s What Comes in Each Edition
summary_en: Preorders have dropped for Star Wars Zero Company, which is set to be released on August 27. We've broken down what comes in each edition and how much they cost.
body_en:
This year's Summer Game Fest was full of exciting reveals. Among the many, many trailers that dropped was Star Wars Zero Company , which showed off a brand new look at the game's story while announcing that preorders are now live ahead of its release date on August 27.
There's a Standard Edition and Deluxe Edition available to preorder at the moment across PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, and PC. Below, you can learn more about what comes with each edition, what they're priced at, and what preorder bonus you'll get for ordering early.
PS5 (Physical)
Xbox Series X (Physical)
Digital
PC
Star Wars Zero Company's Standard Edition is priced at $59.99 for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X copies (both physical and digital), however, PC users can actually get the game for a little cheaper at $49.99.
No matter which copy you order early, you'll get a preorder bonus alongside the main game called the Crystalline Astromech Cosmetic Pack. Below, you can learn more about what's included in it.
Digital
PC
The Deluxe Edition of Star Wars Zero Company appears to be digital only, and is priced $10 higher than the Standard Edition. PlayStation and Xbox storefronts have it for $69.99, while PC players can grab it for $59.99
Alongside the main game and preorder bonus, this edition comes with a wealth of extras for players. You can see them in the image above, but here's a breakdown of what's included, per EA's website :
Both the Standard and Deluxe versions of Star Wars Zero Company come with a cosmetic pack preorder bonus called the Crystalline Astromech Cosmetic Pack. Per EA , this features "the R3 droid, translucent crystalline astromech heads for the R4 and R5 variants, and the BR-1 droid debuting in Star Wars Zero Company."
Star Wars Zero Company is an XCOM-style strategy game that's perfect for those who love a bit of turn-based tactics. Set during the Clone Wars, you play as Hawks, "a disgraced Republic Officer who’s now leading Zero Company through 'a war you don’t see, the one fought in the shadows. The war beneath the war'," per EA's introductory post for the game.
You'll have quite the crew with you, too. Continuing on from EA's post , "An unconventional outfit of professionals for hire hailing from across the galaxy, the members of Zero Company span multiple classic Star Wars archetypes, ranging from astromechs to Jedi. This unlikely ensemble includes a surprising combination of natural teammates and uneasy allies, but they will have to set aside their differences to stop a new threat that, if left unchecked, would consume the galaxy."
If you're curious to get a deeper look into Star Wars Zero Company, check out our IGN Live 2026 interview with the creators .
Hannah Hoolihan is a freelancer who writes with the guides and commerce teams here at IGN.
